children.heading: Ambulatory prenatal care
children.label: a Ambulatory prenatal care
children.text: A State plan approved under may provide for making ambulatory prenatal care available to a pregnant woman during a presumptive eligibility period.section 1396a of this title

children.label: A
children.text: begins with the date on which a qualified provider determines, on the basis of preliminary information, that the family income of the woman does not exceed the applicable income level of eligibility under the State plan, and

children.label: i
children.text: the day on which a determination is made with respect to the eligibility of the woman for medical assistance under the State plan, or

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of a woman who does not file an application by the last day of the month following the month during which the provider makes the determination referred to in subparagraph (A), such last day; and

children.label: A
children.text: is eligible for payments under a State plan approved under this subchapter,

children.label: B
children.text: provides services of the type described in subparagraph (A) or (B) of or in ,section 1396d(a)(2) of this titlesection 1396d(a)(9) of this title

children.label: C
children.text: is determined by the State agency to be capable of making determinations of the type described in paragraph (1)(A), and

children.label: I
children.text: section 254b or 254c of this title,

children.label: II
children.text: subchapter V of this chapter, or

children.label: III
children.text: title V of the Indian Health Care Improvement Act [ et seq.];25 U.S.C. 1651

children.label: I
children.text: , orsection 1786 of this title

children.label: II
children.text: section 4(a) of the Agriculture and Consumer Protection Act of 1973;

children.label: iii
children.text: participates in a State perinatal program; or

children.label: iv
children.text: is the Indian Health Service or is a health program or facility operated by a tribe or tribal organization under the Indian Self-Determination Act () [ et seq.].Public Law 93–63825 U.S.C. 5321

children.heading: Duties of State agency, qualified providers, and presumptively eligible pregnant women
children.label: c Duties of State agency, qualified providers, and presumptively eligible pregnant women
A
children.text: such forms as are necessary for a pregnant woman to make application for medical assistance under the State plan, and

children.label: B
children.text: information on how to assist such women in completing and filing such forms.

children.label: A
children.text: notify the State agency of the determination within 5 working days after the date on which determination is made, and

children.label: B
children.text: inform the woman at the time the determination is made that she is required to make application for medical assistance under the State plan by not later than the last day of the month following the month during which the determination is made.

A pregnant woman who is determined by a qualified provider to be presumptively eligible for medical assistance under a State plan shall make application for medical assistance under such plan by not later than the last day of the month following the month during which the determination is made, which application may be the application used for the receipt of medical assistance by individuals described in section 1396a()(1)(A) of this title.l

children.heading: Ambulatory prenatal care as medical assistance
children.label: d Ambulatory prenatal care as medical assistance
A
children.text: during a presumptive eligibility period,

children.label: B
children.text: by a provider that is eligible for payments under the State plan; and
is included in the care and services covered by a State plan;

children.heading: Option to provide presumptive eligibility
children.label: e Option to provide presumptive eligibility
children.text: If the State has elected the option to provide a presumptive eligibility period under this section or , the State may elect to provide a presumptive eligibility period (as defined in subsection (b)(1)) for individuals who are eligible for medical assistance under clause (i)(VIII), clause (i)(IX), or clause (ii)(XX) of subsection (a)(10)(A) or in the same manner as the State provides for such a period under this section or , subject to such guidance as the Secretary shall establish.section 1396r–1a of this title1section 1396u–1 of this titlesection 1396r–1a of this titleSo in original. Probably means subsection (a)(10)(A) of .1section 1396a of this title
